description: PAYDAY 2: Criminal Dawn is a roguelite conversion for PAYDAY 2 that was built to support Archipelago.

v0.0.0.description:
First release! This APWorld **requires** [launcher version 0.6.7](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases/latest) to function, so make sure you're using it.

I wouldn't personally recommend bringing this APWorld to any public sessions right now since it's still very WIP, but should be pretty functional. There will likely be bugs and balance issues.

I ***highly*** recommend reading the [FAQ](https://github.com/CATEM1NAL/PD2-Criminal-Dawn/blob/main/FAQ.md) before you start playing.
v0.0.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.0.0

v0.0.1.description:
This update is compatible with worlds generated on the previous version (though I doubt there's any).

### Mod changes:
- Fixed various game crashes.
- Removed coin duplication (used for testing).

### APWorld changes:
- Scrungle is a little less overzealous about sending checks.
- Other back-end client improvements.

please read the [faq](https://github.com/CATEM1NAL/PD2-Criminal-Dawn/blob/main/FAQ.md) before asking questions kthxbye
v0.0.1.title: Criminal Dawn v0.0.1

v0.0.2.description:
Yesterday was a rushed release and very broken. Whoops. Maybe this update makes up for that:

### Mod changes:
- Crew AI unlocks are available from the start. They were already free, now you just start with them.
  - This change was also going to be applied to weapon mods (making them all permanently available), but I wasn't able to get it working for this update. Weapon mods *are* however free, and you can just unlock them.
- Started changing the game's colour scheme to further solidify the mod as its own thing with its own visual identity.
  - Heist briefing is still unfinished; I'm struggling to get a look that I'm 100% happy with.
- Changed a ton of text to make it clearer how the mod works (these changes *may* be overwritten if you have another mod that changes text, currently unaware of a way to force Criminal Dawn's text).
- Set up a system for fixing the broken unlock names. If you find any that I didn't catch, please let me know!
- Removed the singular starting coin.
- Disabled checks coming through immediately after buying rooms in the safehouse due to unlock menu jank (will be re-enabled at some point in future). Make sure you go to the inventory after buying safehouse rooms to properly receive your items!

### APWorld changes:
- New worlds generate with the correct number of coins for both run lengths.
- Fixed safehouse logic being totally scuffed.
- Scrungle now gets executed when changing server.

There may be a bug where the client sends heist completed checks upon connecting to a new multiworld, not entirely sure though.
v0.0.2.title: Criminal Dawn v0.0.2

v0.1.0.description:
Getting closer and closer to an **actually playable**¹ experience!

This update **will not** be compatible with previously generated worlds due to changes to how difficulty scaling works.

### Mod changes:
- **New Unlocks:** Added _snowball_, _musket_, _cash blaster_, and _ham-mer_ to the item pool.
  - If you're struggling with the difficulty then the cash blaster should alleviate that, if you manage to get it.
95 damage at 2000 rof deletes anything it comes into contact with (including your friends, so be careful!).
- **Balancing:** Difficulty scaling is now smoother with final difficulties below Death Wish.
- **UX:** All weapon mods and masks (_that you have DLC for_) are unlocked by default.
  - Card drops can only be cash, XP, or mask customisation items now.
- **UX:** Upgrade menu replaces the skill and perk menus (`Options > Mod Keybinds > Criminal Dawn` to view menu in-game!).
- **Fix:** Drop in players no longer crash.
- **Fix:** Telemetry no longer crashes on fresh saves. I deleted an entire function from the game. I'm sure this won't cause any problems.
- **Fix:** Safe house rooms that require licensed content (removed in 2023 and 2024) are now visible to players that bought the game after licensed content was disabled. You still don't own the missing content though, sorry :(

### APWorld changes:
- **Generation:** Tweaked difficulty scaling.
- **YAML:** Added final difficulties below Overkill.
- **YAML:** Increased max number of primaries, secondaries, throwables, and melees by 1 to account for event items.
- **YAML:** Decreased the minimum score checks from 120 to 100.
Increased the max score checks to 400. I don't recommend more than 150 unless you want a *really* long game.
Default is still 120.

### Known issues:
- North Star weapon mods are still locked. Investigating a solution.

*¹ No promises are made about how playable this actually is.*
v0.1.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.1.0

v0.1.1.description:
whoops

### Mod changes:
- Fixed difficulty being set to easy

No APWorld changes.
v0.1.1.title: Criminal Dawn v0.1.1

v0.2.0.description:
These changes are brought to you by **Big Balance**.

Incompatible with previous versions.

### Mod changes:
- **UX:** Completely redid options menu, added mutator modes:
  - **Off:** No random mutators will generate. Friendly Fire (Hard+) and Winters' Shields (Death Wish+) will still be enabled.
  - **On:** Number of mutators will scale with heist number (1 mutator for first heist, 2 for second heist, etc).
  - **On + Scaling (default):** Number of mutators will also scale with items received in the same way as difficulty.
  - **Inverted Scaling:** Number of mutators will start maxed out (based on difficulty setting), but *decrease* as you receive items.
  I can't imagine anyone will actually use this, but it's here anyway!

  As with the heist toggles, feel free to swap between these in-game and get a feel for which one you vibe with the most.
  If difficulty is capped to normal, the scaling options won't really do anything.
- **Balancing:** Winters' Shields removed from mutator pool - they now always activate when playing on Death Wish or Death Sentence. This is to prevent early game runs getting fucked over and negate a late game issue where shields are incredibly weak.
- **Balancing:** Tweaked difficulty scaling again so it's *should* be a lot smoother.
This will also effect the mutator scaling settings!
- **Balancing:** 4 heist mode now starts with a higher score multiplier.
- **Balancing:** Completing heist objectives now gives points.
- **Fix:** Infinite time no longer forces you to play the alternate final heist.
- **Fix:** Hopefully made the RNG more R and less NG.
- **Fix:** Enabled secret PONR timers (they were disabled by accident).

### APWorld changes:
- **Logic:** Adjusted to make the time required for heist completions a bit more lenient.
  - Previously logic was based on each heist taking an average of 10 minutes since that *is* feasible with good RNG, however it could lead to bad generations like the one we got in testing where, on Quick/Short, we were expected to beat two heists in twenty minutes to progress. The heists in the Tier 3/4 pools are so long on average that this just wasn't realistic, so heist completion logic has been changed to expect an average of 15 minutes per heist (matching the number of time items).
  - 4 heist mode now expects you to have at least 15 minutes to beat the first heist as opposed to 10 on 6 heist mode for similar reasons.
- **Fix:** Bots were potentially generating later than intended.
- **Fix:** Armour and deployables don't generate in excess.
v0.2.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.2.0

No APWorld changes.

v0.2.3.description:
Was in the middle of a bigger update, but here, take this:

### Mod changes:
- **UX:** Made various lines of text clearer.
- **Fix:** Safehouse shouldn't be locked on game launch anymore. Still some weird shit going on with how the tiers increase though, needs further investigation.
- **Fix:** Fixed crash in create_ponr.

### APWorld changes:

- **YAML:** Estimation for how long Quick/Short should take is now included (about 8 hours).
v0.2.3.title: Criminal Dawn v0.2.3

v0.2.4.description:
Fi(UX)es.

### Mod changes:
- **UX:** ~~Community items are available without following the community hub. Probably.~~ Didn't work.
- **UX:** Achievement locked weapon mods _should_ now be unlocked even if you don't have the achievements.
- **UX:** Infamy cosmetics are unlocked by default.
- **UX:** All event rewards are now unlocked by default.
  - **Fix:** North Star weapon mods are now available!
  - **Fix:** The Deagle's *Red Rover* exclusive set and the Chimano 88's *Golden Rule* exclusive set are also available.
- **Visual:** Van skins are now randomly chosen.
- **Fix:** ~~Safehouse is maybe less jank.~~ Safehouse descriptions are broken. Functionally, it should be better though!
- **Fix:** The mere presence of the Chimano Compact should no longer cause the unlock menu to break. Probably.
- **Fix:** Mutators will no longer treat the difficulty as if there is no cap (fr fr).
- **Upgrades:** Various adjustments to names/descriptions.

### APWorld changes:
- **YAML:** BigLobby mode now guarantees at least 7 bots instead of 4 (if you're turning this on, you probably want a BIG lobby).

### Known issues:
- Safehouse descriptions are one tier behind when you are on the border of two tiers.
- North Star is unlocked by default.
- Community items are still locked.
v0.2.4.title: Criminal Dawn v0.2.4

v0.3.0.description:
**AUTOMATIC SAVE SWITCHING! A SAFEHOUSE THAT ACTUALLY WORKS! ITEM POOL REWORK! BUY NOW!!!**
This update is incompatible with previous versions, for _hopefully_ obvious reasons.

If you have played the mod before, you will need to reset your save *one* last time and then restart the game for some of these changes to apply correctly. New players shouldn't need to worry about it (I hope...).

### Mod changes:
- **Balance:** Increased cost of all assets. Since money has very little purpose in this mod the intent is to make buying assets more of a strategic decision, but as is tradition the current numbers are likely too far on the punishing end. Feedback is appreciated!
- **UX:** Community items are accessible without following the community hub.
- **UX:** Time reminders are now disabled when you have infinite time.
- **UX:** HUD goes back to vanilla layout when you have infinite time.
- **UX:** Score cap doesn't tell you to get a time upgrade when you have already have all of them.
- **UX:** Clicking perks in the inventory now opens the perk menu instead of the skills menu.
- **UX:** Added placeholder message that shows when completing a run (I'll change it don't worry).
- **UX:** Mask customisation is free!
- **UX:** ALL mask and weapon slots are now unlocked by default. Go nuts.
- **Fix:** Extra starting weapons have gone to the great Item Pool in the sky.
  - Default throwable is now the _Ace of Spades_, as removing the default throwable completely isn't feasible (though I'm sure it's possible).
  - Achievement locked items (_Nova Shank_, _Golden Spoon_, _Wrench_, _Viper Grenade_) are only added to the item pool if you have the required achievement. Planning on changing this so they're available without the achievements, but need to look more into it.
  - The McShay Mod Pack _Ordnance Bag_ is still unlocked by default if you own the DLC. This is intentional as I can't imagine most people would actually pick this over something else, and it's a really nice starting deployable that basically gets replaced as soon as you get another one.
- **Fix:** Non-DLC secondary shotguns are now in the item pool (I must've missed them originally??).
- **Fix:** Safehouse was changed to use my own save system.
- **Fix:** Starting level is 0 again (event jobs give enough XP to reach level 95, apparently).
- **Fix:** Forced all items to have a quantity of 2 again (weapon stat boosts were dropping from cards).
- **Fix:** Masks are no longer an infinite money glitch.
- **UI:** Changed some unlock messages so they read a bit better.

### APWorld changes:
- **Generation:** Infinite time upgrade exists again.
- **Client:** Now automatically handles switching between saves from different slots/seeds. No more resetting your save every seed! You need to restart the game after doing this because the save is only loaded on game launch, and may want to clear out the save folder from time to time (they're not that big though so you can probably get away with leaving it for a while).
  - As a side effect of this, inventory items now **persist** between seeds. You can't equip them until unlocking them again, but you don't need to set up your weapons *every* single time you unlock the same one now.
  - Another side effect, offshore and levels now persist between seeds. You can use this as a fun way to track how much you've played, and can even go infamous (assuming you play that much)!
- **Client:** Added /score command which tells you the current score, score cap, how close you are to the next check, and your total completion of all the score checks.
- **Client:** Additional deathlink messages. You should keep failing heists to see them all!
- **YAML:** Updated max values for item generation and changed descriptions to reflect new non-DLC limits.
v0.3.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.3.0

v0.3.1.description:
No update is complete without new bugs to fix, otherwise I'd be out of a job!

### Mod changes:
- **Fix:** Infamy rewards now unlock correctly.
- **Fix:** Deployable upgrades are given out again.

No APWorld changes.
v0.3.1.title: Criminal Dawn v0.3.1

v0.4.0.description:
_As I spin and spin the world gets blurred
Where am I now? I thought I heard
The YAML is simplified? What does that mean?
Where have my options gone? Is this a dream?_

This update is a major update to the YAML, world gen, and adds new game modes which *should* take less time to complete.
With these changes the world is in a spot where I'm a lot more comfortable recommending people try it out, since the modes should now cover a range of play times and be a lot more foolproof (unless you pick a DLC campaign without owning the DLC. There's nothing I can do to stop you from doing that).

Most of the options from the YAML were removed or merged, so make sure you generate a new one!

As there are a lot of changes to account for the different game modes, there are almost definitely some errors that slipped through since not a whole lot of time has gone into testing each individual mode.

Goes without saying, this is NOT compatible with previous versions.

### Mod changes:
- **Options:** Difficulty cap has moved from the YAML to the in-game mod options, meaning you can now change it at any time to try and find the right balance for you. The world itself doesn't factor in difficulty for generation, so the setting wasn't really doing anything.
- **Balance:** Now receive **3** coins per item instead of **2** (8 items needed for a full tier, down from 12).
  This is actually more of a generation change (which is why it was done), but it *does* affect the way the way the mod plays.
- **Balance:** Per run difficulty now scales gradually to the cap, instead of increasing every heist (for example if the cap is set to Hard, on a 6 heist run the difficulty will increase on the 3rd heist instead of immediately on the second heist). In practice this means that lower difficulty caps will scale smoother, while higher difficulty caps will scale steeper - on 4 heist mode Death Wish now starts on hard and increases to Overkill for the second heist. Difficulty will still scale with items collected on top of this!
- **Heists:** Removed duplicated endless heists from heist pools.
- **Fix:** Changing between saves now resets the equipped deployable if it's invalid.
  - I am legitimately not sure why the game doesn't verify this. Theoretically if you equip the ordnance bag and then disable the McShay Mod Pack in vanilla, it should stay equipped even when the DLC is disabled. Starbreeze things I guess.
- **Fix:** Tutorials no longer show up if you have someone else in the lobby (they're kinda broken in multiplayer).
- **Fix:** Post-run heists are now chosen from all available tiers, instead of just tier 1.
- ~~**UI:** Captain Winters now disables the hostage counter.~~

### APWorld changes:
- **Client:** Improved [Universal Tracker](https://archipelago.miraheze.org/wiki/Universal_Tracker) support (thank @jordansds for this)!
  - Glitch logic is now used to show checks that you can *technically* get to, but aren't currently expected to reach.
  - Now supports YAML-less generation, so if someone else connects to the same slot they can actually see the tracker!
- **Generation:** Tweaks were made to perma-upgrade logic.
- **Generation:** Reduced coin items as part of steps taken to negate fill errors.
- **YAML:** Completely reworked and _drastically_ simplified. Previous option for score checks have been combined with run length to create game modes, and the item generation options have become an axe victim (you're guaranteed at least 5 of every weapon type, with extras generating based on available locations). This might seem more limiting from a YAML standpoint, but it makes the setup experience easier for new players and allows me to provide complete, catered packages that I know actually generate and are built for a specific purpose.
  - **Short Day** (75 score checks) and **Long Day** (120 score checks) are the original 4 and 6 heist modes.
  - **Pointless Day** is a mode built purely around playing random heists and getting points. With only 2 safehouse tiers and 85 score checks, you win after maxing out your score.
    - This mode is meant to be a quicker, lower stress alternative to Short Day (just keep playing until you send all your checks).
  - **Moving Day** is a mode that was built as a proof of concept for the new game mode system. You play random heists until you get enough coins to fully upgrade the safehouse, and then you win. There are 130 score checks because this mode is a fill error nightmare.
    - This mode was made as a test and will receive no support. It probably sucks (blame @jordansds for this)!
  - Campaigns are a set of new game modes that all use the same core rules: 80 score checks, one safehouse tier, and a fixed heist order. The game will refuse to load the heist if you don't own the DLC, so make sure you own all the necessary content before playing a campaign!
    - **Return of the Rat:** Hector heists in chronological order.
    - **Murky Day:** Assorted Murkywater heists, starting with Shadow Raid.
    - **I Need My Payday Too:** Dentist heists in chronological order.
    - **Greatest Heist Of All:** Final story arc (Reservoir Dogs to White House).
    - **Silk Road:** Mexico heists.
    - **City of Gold:** The LA heists, excluding Ukrainian Prisoner (due to weirdness with the heist's PONR timer).
    - **Texas Heat:** Texas heists, excluding Lost in Transit (due to weirdness with the heist's PONR timer).
    - **Classics:** The 6 launch heists from PDTH.
  - Since run length and score checks are now tied together, Glacial pacing adds an additional 25 checks to any mode while BigLobby adds a further 20.
    - If you want the old 4 heist mode (4 heists with 100 score checks, about 8 hours of play time), you can still achieve this by enabling Glacial.
v0.4.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.4.0

v0.4.1.description:
The heist changes here may have *major* consequences on run length, as you don't need to do multiple days to finish a single heist any more. In practice this will probably just means every heist will be a more comparable length, but it might mean the time bonus generation needs to be adjusted to compensate. We'll see!

### Mod changes:
- **Heists:** Split multi days into single day heists. New single days are in the heist toggle menu.
  - **Tier 1:** Biker Heist day 2 has been added.
  - **Tier 2:** Watchdogs can be either day 1 or day 2.
  - **Tier 3:** Reservoir Dogs is now just day 2 (day 1? (the diamond store!)).
  Election Day is always day 2, either the right truck or wrong truck.
  Rats has been removed, Cook Off has been left in its place.
  The first day of Biker Heist, Hotline Miami, and Goat Sim are here.
  - **Tier 4:** Framing Frame is now just day 3.
  Firestarter is now just day 1.
  Hotline Miami and Goat Sim day 2 are here.
  - **Tier 5:** Hoxton Breakout can be either day 1 or 2.
  Big Oil is just day 2. Note that this also means you don't have *any* assets - no plane keys here!
- **Fix:** Resolved crash when opening crew management menu. Weirdly I wasn't getting this crash myself, but the fix *seems* to have worked.
- **Fix:** Readded safehouse hint explaining how the tier levelling works (I'm an idiot).
- **Fix:** Correct number of coins is now loaded on game launch.

No APWorld changes.
v0.4.1.title: Criminal Dawn v0.4.1

v0.4.2.description:
THROWABLES, ARMOURS, AND DEPLOYABLES NOW ACTUALLY GENERATE. I AM STUPID. PLEASE UPDATE TO HAVE A PLAYABLE EXPERIENCE.

### Mod changes:
- **Fix:** Fixed missing grenade unlock text.
- **Fix:** Timer starts at the same time for all players in a multiplayer session again.

### APWorld changes:
- **Fix:** Items actually generate correctly.
- **Game Modes:** Campaign score checks increased from **80** to **90** to account for the extra items being generated.
v0.4.2.title: Criminal Dawn v0.4.2

v0.4.4.title: Criminal Dawn v0.4.4

v0.5.0.description:
*My YAML is back, hip hip hooray
Does this mean I can finally play
The safe house mode, my favourite game
Wait. It's gone. Who do I blame?*

Major APWorld generation changes again, this time to mostly unfuck the fuckery I did in the last version (which was my own fault because I forgot YAML presets exist).

Almost definitely incompatible with prior APWorlds.

### Mod changes:
- **Balancing:** Readjusted difficulty scaling *again* to make it smoother. ***Again.***
- **Fix:** Heist completion gives the correct amount of points to the host.
- **Fix:** Added hard caps to all items so they can't go out of range (possible by spawning items or giving yourself starting items).

### APWorld changes:
- **YAML:** Split game modes back into individual options, now with a bunch of safeguards in place to make sure generation can't get fucky. Game modes still exist as presets (`Archipelago/Players/Templates/Presets`) and I'd still recommend starting from one of these as a base (if you use options creator, [refer to the FAQ](https://github.com/CATEM1NAL/PD2-Criminal-Dawn/blob/main/FAQ.md#recommended-settings-for-each-goal)). The default YAML template is same as the 4 heist preset.
  - The reason I didn't set it up like this in v0.4.0 is because, to be honest, I forgot that presets exist.
  - Solo worlds may fail to generate sometimes (I think this has been an issue for a while, though) - assuming your YAML isn't totally fucked up, just try generating a few times and it should work.
    - The score goal will basically always fail to generate if you max out the safe house tiers, but *only* when generating as a solo world.
- **YAML:** BigLobby now always generates **19** bots.
  - The full 21 was consistently causing generation failures, and 19 sounds like it'll be more than enough...
- **YAML:** Added option to force a bot to generate in sphere 1.
- **Generation:** Added weighting to weapon generation so you don't end up with a million akimbos. Primaries and secondaries are now prioritised.
- **Generation:** Reduced base number of stat boosts from 13 to 7 (extras still generate at random like before).
- **Generation:** After an absolutely gruelling world where the first armour unlock was behind *safehouse tier 4*, you are now guaranteed at least 1 armour unlock in sphere 1.
- **Game Modes:** Safe House mode has been deleted.
  - This mode was never meant to be added in the first place and it's a logic/generation nightmare. I never wanted this. I'm glad it's gone. Fuck safe house mode.
v0.5.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.5.0

v0.5.1.description:
The classic post-update hotfix patch!

### Mod changes:
- **Greatest Heist Of All (Campaign):** Changed first heist from both days of Reservoir Dogs to just the diamond store.
  - Similar change may be implemented for other campaigns in future, but splitting the multidays will require also updating the APWorld.
- **Fix:** Mutators should no longer treat the difficulty as being one higher than it actually is.
- **Fix:** Timer should no longer give infinite time in edge cases where it shouldn't.
- **Fix:** Completing a campaign should no longer crash.

No APWorld changes.
v0.5.1.title: Criminal Dawn v0.5.1

v0.5.2.description:
### Mod changes:
- **Fix:** Score mode doesn't start with a high multiplier.
- **Fix:** Points to next check now shows correct number instead of 0 on check boundary (regressed in 0.4.0).

No APWorld changes.
v0.5.2.title: Criminal Dawn v0.5.2

v0.6.0.description:
_Dallas, I've got a feeling we're not in Payday 2 anymore..._
Due to the absurd list of changes, this version is _absolutely_ not compatible with prior releases.

Check the [updated FAQ](https://github.com/CATEM1NAL/PD2-Criminal-Dawn/blob/main/FAQ.md#are-there-any-core-gameplay-changes-i-should-know-about) for a condensed(ish) list of all the gameplay changes!

### Mod changes:
#### Upgrades:
- **Kickstarter** is now guaranteed to work every time.
- **Repair System** now guarantees that drills are auto restarters.
- **Drill Sawgeant** also applies to computer hacks and has been completely reworked:
  - It has been renamed to **OVKL ToolSys**.
  - ToolSys is no longer a multiworld item - instead, it gives a **2%** speed increase per progression item obtained (up to **99%**).
  - Higher levels of ToolSys also reduce the number of times that drills and saws can jam.
- **Nine Lives** now gives you one extra down per upgrade, and has **8** levels (for a total of nine lives).
- **Rise Above** has been completely reworked, now giving a fixed **+50** armour per level. The health reduction is also fixed, taking **50** health and decreasing by an additional **25** each level. This prevents it from scaling to absurdity while still proving a bigger benefit to weaker armours, without punishing health builds too much.
- All health boosts are now flat increases instead of percentage increases.
- **Agility Shield** now restores 10% of your max armour on dodge, instead of all of it.
- Changed internal typing of upgrades that improve other perks so that they are hopefully more common.
- Added second level of medic bag/ammo bag quantities, giving **4** deployables instead of **2**.
- Added second level of shaped charge quantity, giving **9** shaped charges.
- Added first level of FAK quantity giving only **7** FAKs.
- Added new FAK upgrade that gives them a **50%** chance to restore a down on use. This will make a bit more sense if you continue reading.
- Various fixes to incorrect, misleading, or inconsistent descriptions.

#### Gameplay:
- Timer system has been completely reworked. Instead of receiving time items which also increase the score cap, you now gain extra time and the score cap increases for each progression item received. With 6 heists the timer caps at around 50 minutes now.
  - I *have not* thoroughly tested the new timer values. I know that 6 heist mode is playable (it can be a bit tight at the end!), however shorter run lengths may have incredibly tight timer expectations or even be impossible. Let me know how you feel about the new time system!
  - Infinite time setting now applies when you receive all your progression items, instead of on the final score check.
- I broke stamina. Stamina upgrades have been removed from the pool. Bye bye stamina!
- Regular assets (not preplanning assets) have had their price scaling adjusted to match preplanning assets. In other words, they cost more.
- Downs now persist between heists, and you recover one after each heist. If your max downs are 5 and you finish a heist with 2 left, you'll start the next heist with 3. Do I bring shaped charges to blow open the trucks, or do I bring a doctor bag to restore my downs?
- Doctor bags now only restore one down per charge (base of **3** charges instead of **2**).
- Being traded out of custody revives you with no extra downs. Be careful!
  - You also get 3 seconds of immunity, which was added to prevent friendly fire from instantly killing you again but is a nice bonus in general!
- Lighter armours now regen faster, while heavier armours regen slower. Flak Jacket, Combined Tactical Vest, and Improved Combined Tactical Vest are immune to suppression.
- Tooth and Claw has been reworked accordingly - instead of always regenerating your armour 1.5s after it breaks, it is twice as fast as your regen speed. If your regen speed is 6 seconds, it will regen in 3 seconds. This cannot go any lower than 1.5s, otherwise it gets *very* silly.
- Enemy health is now the same across all difficulties (based on vanilla **Overkill**), but with a lower headshot/crit multiplier.
- Damage grace period has been reworked, now only applying on armour break and varying per difficulty. All damage will otherwise be applied instantly, greatly punishing playing out of cover.
- Enemy damage across all difficulties is now based on **Hard** values, with some tweaks for more problematic enemies to bring them in line.
  - Enemy damage fall-off for all difficulties is now based on **Overkill**/**Mayhem** values (yes, including Death Sentence - you won't get sniped across the map any more).
- Snipers cannot penetrate player armour and don't have lasers on Mayhem+.
- Cloaker cuffing is now enabled by default, as it only really punishes Inspire (which you're won't have most of the time). In all other cases, cloaker cuffing is actually easier to play around as the interaction is faster than reviving and you automatically break free after 60 seconds. Very Hard+ has a chance to *disable* this mutator, instead of enabling it.
- Base player health has been reduced slightly to 200.
- Dodge has been reworked. It is no longer random, instead being deterministic - if you have 25% dodge, you will dodge one shot and then take 3 shots. If you have 50% dodge, you will dodge one shot and then take the next. If you have 75% dodge, you will dodge two shots and then take one shot.
  - Going above 100% dodge doesn't make you immune to damage, either - for every shot you dodge in a row, you gain a stacking 5% dodge penalty. This resets upon taking damage.
- Damage absorption can no longer reduce damage to 0.
- Look. I get it. Stealing loot is hard enough as is, and now you have to defend your bags while all this shit is going on? Good news - cops don't steal your bags any more.

#### Intermission:
These patch notes are long. Let's take a little pit stop, shall we?

These gameplay changes are very clearly *not* PAYDAY 2. Fans of the series might note that some of these changes are inspired by PAYDAY: The Heist / PAYDAY 3, and you'd be correct! I'm one of the lunatics that actually enjoyed the early days of PAYDAY 3, though I will admit, I can take or leave some of the systems and changes that game made. It was nice to have a game that remained grounded and was hard again, much like PAYDAY: The Heist. The name and whole image of this mod is even based on early marketing for PAYDAY 3 ("a new criminal dawn" was a tag line they dropped *incredibly* quickly).

The series is, in my opinion, in a bit of a tricky spot due to its age and how much the gameplay has evolved over time. Long time fans like myself tend to prefer the more focused gameplay from PD:TH and early PAYDAY 2, while newer fans tend to enjoy the power fantasy that PAYDAY 2 currently provides.

Criminal Dawn now fits somewhere between the two ends of the scale, being slow in the early-game but speeding up and leaning more into PAYDAY 2's power fantasy in the late-game.

I *entirely* expect that this update will cause some alienation, however I want to create more distance between vanilla and Criminal Dawn to more clearly define this mod as its own thing. While a lot of the changes here will inevitably make the game harder, I hope some of the lower difficulty considerations are able to keep it enjoyable and fair for people that don't want to be challenged.

If the lower difficulties are too hard for you, let me know! I can add additional mod options to make it easier if need be.

#### Fixes:
- **Jack of all Trades** now persists, instead of only being active for the first heist after getting it.
- Pressing the upgrade menu button multiple times reopens the menu instantly, instead of queuing another menu.
- Finishing a run now automatically sends the completion check, instead of waiting until the next time the mod writes a save.
- Finally tracked down and removed achievement milestone coins.
  - I also replaced the main function responsible for giving out coins so it *only* works for coins given by the mod. If there are any other parts of the vanilla game that I missed which give coins, well, they're also gone now!
- Gutted the part of the game responsible for detecting when players place too many deployables, allowing the 4 ECM upgrade to work in multiplayer as well as the new doctor bag/ammo bag quantity upgrades.

#### Misc:
- **UI:** "THERE IS NO ESCAPE" button has been replaced with an abandon run button. This button takes you back to the lobby but instantly ends your current run.
- **UI:** Combined all new item notifications into a single chat message, instead of them all being individual messages.
- **UI:** Replaced placeholder victory message with an actual victory message.
- **UI:** Post-game now uses the score goal play button ("Heist X" instead of "X/Y").
- **UX:** Mod now loads client data on launch, so you don't need to click "Load Client Data" before playing anymore. Connect in the client, launch the game, and you're good to go!
- **UX:** Mod now remembers the heists you played in the last run, and will avoid picking them in your next run.
- **UX:** Random heist mode (active in score goal and post-game) now prevents repeat heists from being picked. This does technically mean it's possible to _completely_ empty the heist pool if you get a run that lasts long enough, and the game will congratulate you for doing this... by crashing.
- **Campaign:** I Need My Payday Too has had Hotline Miami split into two heists.
- **Campaign:** New Vlad campaign, *You Guys No Fun*, has been added. It consists entirely of free Vlad heists, no DLC required!
- **Matchmaking:** Keys have been updated, preventing players on different slots from connecting to each other. Now only players on the same slot can join each other.
- **Localization:** All the required work to make the mod fully translatable into other languages is now complete. I have no idea *if* anyone will want to do this, but it's possible!

### APWorld changes:
- **Logic:** Heavily reworked. Time upgrades no longer exist (tied to total progression items instead).
  - Progression items are skills, perks, perma-skills, perma-perks, extra lives, and extra bots (with BigLobby active only 3 of them count).
- **Generation:** Drill Sawgeant no longer exists.
- **Generation:** Six more Nine Lives items have been added.
- **YAML:** Progression pacing is gone. With the changes to how time works, it didn't really make sense to keep it around - how quickly you progress is now more closely tied to the speed at which you receive items. This may also help somewhat in making the length of the world be based more on the other games in the multiworld, so it might perform a bit better in quicker settings now.

### Known issues:
- The faster computer hacks can cause quirky behaviour. The game expects hacks to last a fixed amount of time, so dialogue can play out of order.
- If two players in the same lobby desync their ToolSys levels, drill/saw timers will be different for each player.
  - The conditions for this to happen are _so incredibly specific_ that you need to be trying to break the game or playing in a stupid way for this to occur in the first place. In practicality, this isn't an issue and will never happen - but it *can* happen.
- Downs for other players are visibly capped at 5 in multiplayer. No clue why, but it's not a major issue.

That's everything! Due to how major some of the changes were, there are almost definitely new bugs caused by rewriting large chunks of the mod/APWorld that will need resolving, but given how long I've had to work on this update I *think* I've caught most of the major ones already... there will definitely be some things that I missed though, or functionality that I forgot to reimplement correctly.

With all the gameplay changes, it's also pretty likely some upgrades will be broken - either making the game too easy, or not working as intended. If a particular perk/skill combo breaks the game, I'll likely leave it (synergies are fun!). If it is *completely* game breaking or ruins the experience too much then I'll take action on it, but try out the new Criminal Dawn and let me know what you think!
v0.6.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.0

v0.6.1.description:
These fixes sure are hot!

### Mod changes:
- Fixed Rats not being counted as owned (Hector campaign).
- Fixed Flak Jacket, CTV, and ICTV making you immune to damage.
- Fixed some peer crashes.
- Tweaks to new item received message.

No APWorld changes.
v0.6.1.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.1

v0.6.10.description:
This update brings some pretty big improvements to upgrade generation behind the scenes, and fixes a bug that has existed ever since the mod was released (enabling some perks that have never been accessible)!

### Mod changes:
- Altered the upgrade selection process, replacing upgrade categories with rarities instead. This means the stronger upgrades should now be a little less common, and the upgrade pools should be a bit more random.
  - Each upgrade has an assigned rarity which currently is not visible in-game (not sure how to display rarity without it being cluttered). The chances are 10% for a rare upgrade, 25% for an uncommon upgrade, and 65% for a common upgrade.
- Finally caught and squished a long standing bug that made upgrade-upgrades never generate. This primarily affects the consumable perks, where previously you could receive the item but the game would never attempt to generate the upgrades associated with it, like healing/dodge for the pocket ECM or Stoic's armour-to-health conversion.
- Getting a consumable perk (leech ampule, kingpin injector, stoic flask, etc) will now disable all other consumable perks.
- The captain spawning on Alaskan Deal is now affected by Objectivity, so you don't have to sit around for 30 seconds waiting for him to pop into existence.
- Fixed multiplayer timer crash (introduced in v0.6.6).

No APWorld changes since v0.6.8.
v0.6.10.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.10

v0.6.11.description:
### Mod changes:
- Minor text changes to improve clarity/formatting.
- More heists are now affected by Objectivity, and added some elements that were previously unaffected:
  - Shacklethorne escape helicopter.
  - Panic Room snipers.
  - Henry's Rock.
  - Green Bridge plane.
  - No Mercy elevator (can now be completed in 5 minutes).
- Fixed some objectives that weren't sped up:
  - Undercover hack (can now be completed in 5 minutes).
  - Birth of Sky helicopters (can now be completed in 7 minutes).
  - Crude Awakening oil pump (can now be completed in 10 minutes).

### APWorld changes:
- Added time estimates to preset names. There have been no functionality changes since v0.6.8, so updating isn't required.
v0.6.11.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.11

v0.6.12.description:
ERROR: crimdawn_v0_6_12_desc

### Mod changes:
- Bomb: Dockyard is now sped up (and fixed the broken computer hack (hopefully)).
- Completely restructured the base localisation file. Instead of being multiple files, it's now one big file - while this might sound worse due to how much text this mod adds, it's actually a lot better because I also swapped from using a JSON to a YAML so I can actually structure the file in a sane way. On the surface this shouldn't change anything, but it fixes an issue where non-localised languages (any non-English language right now) would fail to fallback to English for most text. As simply as possible, **you can play in your native language and you won't see errors now**.

No functional APWorld changes since v0.6.8.
v0.6.12.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.12

v0.6.2.description:
### Mod changes:
- First saw is no longer given immediately upon launching the game.
- Fixed game crash when launching before connecting to a multiworld for the first time.
- Removed some debug log text that was (potentially) causing performance issues.
- Thermite in Big Bank and First World Bank are affected by speed upgrades.
- Fuel on Alaskan Deal is affected by speed upgrades.
- Meth cooking on heists where it's required is affected by speed upgrades.
- Circle cutter on Brooklyn Bank is affected by speed upgrades.
- Plane on GO Bank is affected by speed upgrades.
- Refuelling the helicopter in Beneath the Mountain is affected by speed upgrades.
- White House is no longer affected by speed upgrades to bring the amount of time it takes more in line with Crude Awakening (plus it's kinda lame to just skip every objective in the final heist, you wanna actually *play* it right?).
  - With hindsight this may be impossible with current timer values?? If it isn't doable, let me know and I'll tweak some more values.
  - Speed upgrades *will* apply to White House when playing the score goal or after finishing a run.
- Safehouse prompt will no longer appear after going infamous.

The elements now being affected by speed upgrades haven't been tested super thoroughly - I know that it definitely *works* (at least for some of them), but I can't say for sure how it might affect the objective progression. Things may break!

No APWorld changes.
v0.6.2.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.2

v0.6.3.description:
I had a bad headache today. It was really annoying.

### Mod changes:
- Added content from the [Espionage Weapon Pack](https://store.steampowered.com/app/4543600).
  - Spy Camera is a default deployable.
  - Laser Chronometer is part of the saw pool.
- Added another round of heist elements that are now affected by speed upgrades:
  - Hotline Miami day 1: Bar code reader and truck that opens the hatch.
  - Beneath The Mountain: Breaching charges.
  - Reservoir Dogs: Nitrogen and C4 drop.
  - Goat Sim day 2: Plane that picks up the cage.
  - Counterfeit: Hose.
  - Heat Street: Gas cans.
  - Mallcrasher: Escape helicopter.
  - Biker Heist day 1: Time between objectives.
  - Jewelry Store: Escape van.
  - Meltdown: Vault temperature.

Again, there may be issues with these new elements as they haven't been tested super thoroughly. Let me know if any of them don't work correctly!

No APWorld changes.
v0.6.3.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.3

v0.6.4.description:
Apparently the last thing I did before taking a week to myself was break Big Bank. Whoops. Updates will resume again soon, other heist objectives still need speeding up.

### Mod changes:
- Fixed crash when loading into Big Bank.

No APWorld changes.
v0.6.4.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.4

v0.6.5.description:
### Mod changes:
- Final score check is now accessible again (score goal is completable).
- Charon's lockpicking on Brooklyn 10-10 is now affected by speed upgrades.
- EMP and required body scan on Boiling Point are now affected by speed upgrades.
- Prison cell timer on Hell's Island is now affected by speed upgrades.

No APWorld changes.
v0.6.5.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.5

v0.6.6.description:
me when I realise the score goal takes 10 hours to complete

### Mod changes:
- Added compatibility with [Bot Weapons and Equipment](https://modworkshop.net/mod/12852).
- Added "Lethal Death Link" as a mod option, which allows received death links to send you into custody (disabled by default and may cause undue suffering).
- Death links are no longer sent when aborting runs or running out of time.
- Fully disabled safehouse raids.
- Recoloured drill screen (was done in v0.6.0 but forgot to include it until now).
- Because speed upgrades also affect objective speed now, OVKL ToolSys has been renamed to Objectivity.
  - The helicopter on Stealing Xmas is now affected by Objectivity.
  - Van on Armoured Transport heists is now affected by Objectivity.
  - Vans and helicopters during escapes are now affected by Objectivity.
  - Hacking the armoury door on Breakfast in Tijuana is now affected by Objectivity.
  - The helicopter on Big Oil is now affected by Objectivity.
- Unlock menu is no longer shown during launch to prevent a potential softlock.

### APWorld changes:
- Back-end client death link changes to make the mod-side changes actually function.
- Added two additional YAML presets based on the 6 and 4 heist modes, which should take less time to finish. Options Creator users can refer to [the new wiki](https://github.com/CATEM1NAL/PD2-Criminal-Dawn/wiki/YAML#presets) to see the settings for these.
- Score goal is now completable.
v0.6.6.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.6

v0.6.8.description:
me when I fix the score goal (allegedly)

### Mod changes:
- Score goal now receives a score multiplier, just like the other modes. This brings the playtime down from 10 hours to 3 hours.
- True random heist selection is now even truer and random-er (you don't have a 16% chance for White House anymore).
- More objectives are affected by Objectivity:
  - White House helicopter escape.
  - Attaching cables on Birth of Sky.
  - Shacklethorne's vault timelock.
  - Safe timer on Cursed Kill Room.
  - Pipe puzzle oil pump on Crude Awakening.
- Tried to improve clarity of how the heist tiering works in the options menu.
- Adjustments to assault break timer - it now lasts between 45 and 60 seconds depending on how long the heist has been loud, and always lasts 60 seconds if you have a hostage. This applies to all difficulties - on Normal this is 15 seconds more than vanilla, while on Death Sentence you get 35 to 45 seconds more than vanilla.
- Agility Shield has been reworked: it now restores 2 HP on dodge. This skill is a balancing nightmare so don't be too surprised if it changes again.

### APWorld changes:
- Possibly fixed death link being broken.
v0.6.8.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.8

v0.6.9.description:
nice.

### Mod changes:
- Added [PUBG weapons](https://store.steampowered.com/news/app/218620/view/693135146596958296) to the item pool!
- Fixed multiday heists appearing in score goal and post-game state.
- Agility Shield now correctly heals 2 HP.
- Fixed some timer elements not being modified. This is a little buggy and any timers with visible indicators will show the *original* timer values, not the modified timer values (e.g. the breaching charge on Beneath the Mountain says 45 seconds, even though it's shorter). This fix affects **Beneath the Mountain**, **Counterfeit**, **Hotline Miami day 1**, **Alaskan Deal**, **Brooklyn 10-10**, **Boiling Point**, **Hell's Island**, and **Breakfast in Tijuana**.
- Additional heist objectives are now affected by Objectivity:
  - Big Bank thermite is now affected again.
  - White House thermite is affected.
  - Big Oil's engine inspection is affected.
  - ~~Breaking Ballot's computer hack is affected.~~ Didn't seem to work.
  - Bomb: Forest's water objective is affected.
  - Breakfast in Tijuana's escape and evidence burning objectives are affected.
  - Aftershock's helicopters are affected.
  - Four Stores' escape van is now affected.
  - The Diamond's timelocks, gas, and helicopter are now affected.

No APWorld changes since v0.6.8.
v0.6.9.title: Criminal Dawn v0.6.9

v0.7.0.description:
The primary change for this update was moving more gameplay options out of the YAML and into the mod options, for easier tweaking and mid-game customisation (if needed). Updating to this version _should_ be safe for existing multiworlds (if you run into any issues it's pretty easy to downgrade!), but any worlds generated on v0.7.x **will not** be backwards compatible with clients and mods running v0.6.x.

### Mod changes:
- Reworked **Lethal Death Link** option into a proper death link toggle. You can now turn death link on, off, or swap to and from lethal mode in-game.
  - Fixed bug where all players in a lobby could send death links simultaneously instead of just the host.
  - Fixed lethal death link so it actually works as intended.
- Added **Infinite Time** toggle to mod options, so you can enable or disable it depending on how you're feeling. Back in the Mesozoic era this used to add an additional Extra Time item, so the generator needed to be aware of it - that's not the case anymore.
- Stealth only heists are now **disabled** by default. If you already disabled these heists, nothing will have changed. If you're a psychopath that actually enjoys getting Shadow Raid in the middle of a run, you will have to re-enable the stealth only heists.
- *Very* minor tweaks to time gained per item for run lengths 3/4/6.
- Score goal now gains 100 seconds per item instead of the _very specific_ 106 (max time reduced by 5 minutes).
- Added Death Of Democracy campaign, featuring Elephant heists.
  - Framing Frame day 3, Election Day bank, Big Oil day 2.
- Added Gun Runners campaign, containing various heists with lootable weapons.
  - Firestarter day 1, Aftershock, Brooklyn 10-10, Bomb: Dockyard, Midland Ranch, Border Crossing.
- Added Stealth Mission campaign. Wh... why???
  - Breakin' Feds, Yacht Heist, Murky Station, Shadow Raid, Car Shop.
  - **This campaign is probably complete agony to play and will likely take many, MANY hours to complete.**
- So, what's the deal with the Ace of Spades? I mean, you get 21 of them. That's not how many should be in a standard deck. This has been corrected.

### APWorld changes:
- Removed death link and infinite time options from the YAML.
v0.7.0.title: Criminal Dawn v0.7.0

v0.7.1.title: Criminal Dawn v0.7.1

v0.7.2.description:
This is _objectively_ a good update.

### Mod changes:
- Fixed hack on Breakin' Feds being sped up.
- Sped up every remaining heist that has not been affected until now.
  - I almost definitely missed some objectives. Please let me know if you find any!

No APWorld changes since v0.7.0.
v0.7.2.title: Criminal Dawn v0.7.2

v0.7.3.description:
call me frankenstein because I'm bringing back the dead

### Mod changes:
- Removed bag pickup cooldown.
- Unschrödingered death links.
- **Objective speed**:
  - Bile arrives quicker on **Hotline Miami day 1**.
  - Forgot to speed up **Watchdogs** in the last patch. Whoops.
- **Body bags**:
  - Fixed **Cleaner** perma-skill so you actually start with a body bag.
    - Body bag stat boosts now require **Cleaner**.
  - Fixed **Winston Wolfe** so it increases your body bag capacity instead of giving you a body bag.
  - Added new **Citizen Bane** stat boost that gives you a second starting body bag.
- **Mutators**:
  - Fixed **Cloaker Arrest** not being disabled when it gets picked.
  - **Waking Nightmare** (all dozers are replaced with titandozers) is now forced on when playing Death Sentence. This was originally a random mutator way back in the day, but was removed due to being not fun at all to play due to how much health dozers had on higher difficulties. Enemy stats being standardised should alleviate that somewhat, and forcing it on Death Sentence gives that difficulty another little quirk to separate it from the rest.
  - I did briefly consider re-adding **Clone Army** into the mutator pool, however it was cut for a reason - it's complete and utter ass.
    - Shields is a complete nightmare even with AP weapons due to the sheer quantity of them.
    - Medics is, on paper, just the heavy SWAT mutator due to a recent game update that prevents medics from being able to heal each other. Even if that wasn't a thing, it sounds incredibly annoying to play.
    - Bulldozers... yeah. I don't need to say anything.
    - Cloak-- ok you get the idea.
    - Re-added **Clone Army - Tasers** to the mutator pool (Hard+). It's effectively just the heavy SWAT mutator but every single SWAT can tase you. Have fun?

No APWorld changes since v0.7.0
v0.7.3.title: Criminal Dawn v0.7.3

v0.7.4.description:
### Mod changes:
- Moved **Clone Army - Tasers** from **Hard** to **Overkill**, and made it *significantly* rarer.
- Tried to fix a potential issue where death links _may_ be able to send multiple times under certain conditions, will require further changes to fix if this doesn't work.

No APWorld changes since v0.7.0.

- Fixed drill jams not being reduced.
- Fixed an off-by-one error in how difficulty scaling is calculated.

No APWorld changes since v0.7.0
v0.7.5.title: Criminal Dawn v0.7.5

v1.0.0.description:
Thanks for all the fish!

_**Due to some changes in the upgrade table, this version does not support old multiworlds. Starting a new multiworld is required.**_

### Mod changes:
- Timer has been extended across all run lengths.
- Fixed **Infinite Time** setting not working.
- Fixed **Ambidexterity** stat boost not working.
- [Backported](https://github.com/CATEM1NAL/PD2-Criminal-Dusk) some balance tweaks:
  - **Excitement** (Maniac perk deck) has been simplified to try and reduce the crazy damage reduction it offers. Instead of stacks converting to absorption after 4 seconds, this now occurs after 2 seconds - however, the decay also occurs at the same time, and removes **100%** of all stacks. In practice this means that hysteria stacks only track the damage dealt in the last 2 second window, giving you team-wide bursts of damage reduction as long as you're dealing damage.
  - Fixed a bug with **Agility Shield** that was causing it to recover percentage health, instead of fixed health. It was also nerfed on top of this, because it was still too strong with dodge.
  - Nerfed **Tooth and Claw**'s fixed armour regen from a minimum of 1.5 seconds (same as vanilla) to 3 seconds. This feels a lot more fair, and prevents some cases where Tooth and Claw make you literally invincible.

### APWorld changes:
- Updated logic to account for the extra time.
- Added two event themed campaigns to the YAML, **Night Of Frights** and **Holiday Special**. A snowball in June?

---

### What's next:
If there are any bugs that pop up, feel free to @ me and let me know. I'll try and get to them when I can, but having spent the last 5 months working on this pretty much every day I think it's about time I work on something else. Originally that was going to be [**Criminal Dusk**](https://github.com/CATEM1NAL/PD2-Criminal-Dusk) (taking all the gameplay changes from Criminal Dawn and turning them into a more vanilla style experience with skill selection), however I've realised that I'm just kinda burnt out on PAYDAY modding and APWorld development.

That's not to say that this hasn't been a great experience, far from it. Its been a lot of fun coming up with gameplay reworks to try and resolve little annoyances that I have with vanilla, or try to figure out how to fit this whole puzzle into the hole of an APWorld. The funniest thing is I didn't actually know anything about lua before working on this either, but it very quickly became appealing to me the more I used it to the point that I am *strongly* considering using it for future projects, if possible.

I can't make any promises for what comes next, but what I can say for sure is I'll be taking a break to work on things that I've wanted to work on for a while. Thanks for sticking around, and thanks for playing this weird little experiment that I wasn't even sure if it was possible to make!
v1.0.0.title: Criminal Dawn v1.0.0

v1.0.1.description:
Download this update to purchase the **Tutorial Heist Pack** for **$6.99\***!

### Mod fixes:
- Ownership check no longer fails for the tutorials.

No APWorld fixes since v1.0.0.

_\* The **Tutorial Heist Pack** is not a real DLC and this mod does not secretly drain your bank account. Yet._
v1.0.1.title: Criminal Dawn v1.0.1
